entry.title: Re: UI5 - Usage of Visual Changes in SAP Fiori Elements
entry.content: @eskender1 that page is for CAP applications and doesn't apply to on-premise. The variants do allow default values, but only constant values, not dynamic like Today.

entry.title: User Propagation from CPI to S/4 Backend
entry.content:
I have a third-party application connecting to our S/4 System using Cloud Connector and CPI. I created an iFlow that is working as a passthrough for SOAP calls to our backend. Currently they are sending the endpoint using a SAP ID credentials and then the receiver (S/4) is being sent using Basic Authentication of a SAP GUI user. The user logs into the 3PA using SAP GUI credentials, and we need that logon to be propagated to the backend system instead of using the static user we are currently. I am not sure how to do this as if I use Principal Propagation with the current set up, it is propagating the SAP ID account that is being sent with the sender request. We are currently using Neo. Any help is appreciated.

*Additional notes: there will be >100 users that will be using this that exist as SAP GUI users and do not have SAP IDs (S#).

entry.title: Re: Solving RSA1 issue after BW Refresh
entry.content:
@avinashkumar2232 maybe it´s just me because i dont know that much about BW, but what is a RSA1 issue?
Your only explanation about the issue is your title, nothing else, can you update that?

entry.title: SAP Advanced Planning - Product Planning error
entry.content:
Hi S/4HANA ePPDS Experts, 

Need your help on below planning error. We have S/4HANA 2023 edition and activated ePPDS. In system, product is planned with product heuristic. Forecast has been maintained for product in product view and then product heuristic run is executed.

After product heuristic planning run, system is giving error.

CIF_ORDER_INBOUND_30A,SY-SUBRC:1

Forecast and planned orders created in S/4HANA ePPDS are not getting transferred to MD04.

At same time, process orders created in advanced planning are getting transferred to S/4HANA (MD04).

Can you please let me know if anything needs to maintained on queue or any data replication.

Thank you in advance.

Regards,

Nilesh

entry.title: Re: Smarttable fields are changeable although they have been defined as not changeable
entry.content: what do you mean editable? You set editable="true", so what do you expect from the table?

entry.title: Re: Sap fiori ListObject extensibility in fiori elements app
entry.content: what do you mean more columns? You can display how many columns you like, if you run out of space it is display at the left when you choose the detailed view, what would you like to achive?

entry.title: Re: Table Grouping in Fiori Elements
entry.content: Maybe better to create a CDS View with the grouping? Bit more overhead but it is easier to implement?

entry.title: Re: New inspection lot created from external ewm

entry.title: Re: Junk value is coming when uploading text file to AL11...
entry.content: Then, probably the file you are trying to read is incorrect, it really contains 3 characters ï»¿ which should not be there, probably there was a mistake when generating the file (OPEN DATASET ... FOR OUTPUT).

entry.title: Stop creation inspection lot for STO in EWM
entry.content:
Hi All,

when Goods receipt is posted for the inbound delivery created from STO inspection lot is created. We don't want to create inspection lot for STO inbound delivery as the batch was already inspected and confirmed in the another plant.

I am working on S4 Hana embedded system 2023. I changed inspection lot summary with Batch/Product in material master but still its creating inspection lot.

Kindly Advise how to stope the IL creation.

Regards,

 

Chand Basha.S

entry.title: Re: Event onSelectionChanged from InteractiveDonutChart d...
entry.content:
You need to add your namespace to the press event.
See here for a example: https://ui5.sap.com/test-resources/sap/fe/core/fpmExplorer/index.html#/customElements/customSectionContent 
See here the documentation https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/topic/a357047be956436ebb1dfebf1aa29af2 

<core:FragmentDefinition xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" xmlns="sap.m" xmlns:l="sap.ui.layout">
	<l:VerticalLayout core:require="{handler: 'SalesOrder/ext/CustomColumn'}">
		<Button text="Custom Button" press="handler.buttonPressed" />
	</l:VerticalLayout>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

I think it works like this as well:

<core:FragmentDefinition 
	xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" 
	xmlns="sap.m"
	xmlns:microchart="sap.suite.ui.microchart" >

	<HBox>

		<microchart:InteractiveDonutChart
			id="idDonutChart"
			selectionChanged="onSelectionChanged"
			press="my.custom.namespace.onSelectionChanged"
			selectionEnabled="true"
			displayedSegments="8"
			segments="{_lgort}">
			<microchart:segments>
				<microchart:InteractiveDonutChartSegment
					value="{QuantityStock}"
					label="{= ${StorageLocation} + ' - ' + ${StorageLocationTxt} }" />
			</microchart:segments>
		</microchart:InteractiveDonutChart>

	</HBox>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

entry.title: Re: Junk value is coming when uploading text file to AL11...
entry.content: the code is below:

entry.title: environment variables In SAP Datasphere
entry.content: In SAP Datasphere, are there environment variables? What are they? How can I use them?

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 - Controller function not call after change the view setting of macros table
entry.content: Sounds like a bug, tried a higher or the latest version? May consider opening a Ticket with SAP

entry.title: Re: Junk value is coming when uploading text file to AL11...
entry.content: the code is: TRY. CALL METHOD cl_abap_file_utilities=>create_utf8_file_with_bom EXPORTING file_name = lv_file. CATCH cx_sy_file_open INTO DATA(lx_open). CATCH cx_sy_file_authority INTO DATA(lx_auth). CATCH cx_sy_file_io INTO DATA(lx_io). CATCH cx_sy_file_close INTO DATA(lx_close) . ENDTRY. IF lv_file IS NOT INITIAL. OPEN DATASET lv_file FOR APPENDING IN TEXT MODE ENCODING DEFAULT . IF sy-subrc = 0 and lv_hdr IS NOT INITIAL. TRANSFER lv_hdr TO lv_file. ENDIF. endif

Thanks, Steven.

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 & OData: Multiple service calls vs single serv...
entry.content:
> When the service is called by the UI5 application, is the data for both entity sets automatically retrieved from the backend or is it just one entity set that is retrieved?

depends on the implementation of the app, possibly yes. here too, take a look at the documentation, keyword batch.

> can I use just one OData service to expose the data to a UI5 application or do I need to create an OData service for each DB table?

if you ask me, there should be one service responsible for an app. it contains everything. some people think differently, i would like to keep the complexity and responsibilities to a minimum. but here too there is documentation and a service for every table is absolute nonsense.

entry.title: Cloud Integration - Getting the Error 'FAILED to start bundle from path:
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

I am trying to build a POC using the Cloud Integration capability of the Integration Suite using my BTP trial account. 

When I try to deploy my POC, which is pretty much just a timer and a content modifier within nothing in it, I get the following error 

[CONTENT][CONTENT_DEPLOY][RuntimeError] : FAILED to start bundle from path: /app/data/deploy/bundle-pickup/IF_Robotics_to_S4HANA.jar

https://a7600a1ftrial.integrationsuite-trial.cfapps.ap21.hana.ondemand.com/shell/design/contentpackage/AccentureS4HIntegrationwithRobotics/integrationflows/IF_Robotics_to_S4HANA

entry.title: Re: Junk value is coming when uploading text file to AL11...
entry.content: The file generation seems OK, it's UTF-8 with BOM. How are you reading/getting the file? NB: my first answer was about the reading of the file (FOR INPUT ENCODING UTF-8 SKIPPING BYTE-ORDER MARK).

entry.title: Re: PostgreSQL on SAP BTP - (how-to) deploy and use pgAdmin web version
entry.content:
hi @email2delli 

The product team does not manage the pgadmin web tool and we are not aware of the admin users being blocked. You may deploy again the tool and setup a new password in the manifest.yml, you will need to register the servers entries again. Be aware that if you are deploying for Kyma (K8s) additional steps are necessary as mentioned in the blog post.

Regards, Antonio

entry.title: Re: Neither the system nor the acount has a printing definition., '' [Message 65030-7]

entry.title: Re: SAP EMS objects
entry.content: Hi Sam,

entry.title: Re: Create/Restore Postgres DB Backup
entry.content:
Hi, 

A database instance can be restored to any point in time within the backup retention period (last restorable time being before the last 15 mins), by creating a new DB instance, using the CF command as below:

cf create-service postgresql-db "<service_plan>" "<service_instance_name>" -c '{"source_instance_id": < >, "restore_time":< >}'
  • source_instance_id: indicates which DB instance data you want to use to restore.​
  • restore_time: indicates a point in time until when the DB instance is restored. Time is expected in ISO8601 format: ‘YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ’

You must restore to the same service_plan as that of the source DB instance, and with the same configurations as that of the source DB instance. The parameters which are not at their default values should be specified in -c during restore, (-c parameters in JSON format).

PS: The product team is on the way to launch new features regarding backups and restore options for PostgreSQL, hyperscaler option, new documentation is being prepared and a set of blog posts will be released also, stay tuned.

Hope this helps.

Regards, Antonio

entry.title: Re: How to do Batch call in RAP Odata with CHANGE_SET enabled for CREATE, UPDATE, and DELETE Operati
entry.content: @Rajesh_Amirinen, did you find the solution to this?

entry.title: Re: SAP EMS objects
entry.content: Hi Sam, in EMS you can set up a provisioning process, which can have steps making calls to other systems, to create objects to fulfill that entitlement. So if you have an EMS entitlement for warranty, or access to a support system for some time, you could use APIs in the provisioning steps, to set up specific objects, or enable specific users in the systems providing the actual fulfillment for the entitlement. So you would have to look for APIs for S4 warranty, if you want to create them from EMS entitlements.

entry.title: Re: SAP EMS objects
entry.content: Hi Sam, in EMS you can set up a provisioning process, which can have steps making calls to other systems, to create objects to fulfill that entitlement. So if you have an EMS entitlement for warranty, or access to a support system for some time, you could use APIs in the provisioning steps, to set up specific objects, or enable specific users in the systems providing the actual fulfillment for the entitlement. So you would have to look for APIs for S4 warranty, if you want to create them from EMS entitlements.

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam 2025 in Krakow, Poland
entry.content: I have recorded this quested and assigned it to a topic expert who will be in touch to discuss logistics.

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam 2025 in Paris, France
entry.content: I believe you've already been in touch with Kevin about this request. I'll enter an issue in our tracking board and connect it with him to ensure it's all being planned.  Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Which KPI's do you have in software development?
entry.content:
Hej Enno,

Very generic question & nice topic for a long Stammtisch discussion 😀

First, what’s to be measured? Performance of the team vs. SW Quality.

Of course that relates somehow, but increasing SWQ is only a part of the teams, well, rating, and the success of your organisation. You know probably Goodhart’s Law, and thats why I am always sceptical when it comes to team/individual KPIs.

So let’s only look at SWQ for now - tech lead perspective. As an engineering manager I will probably look also from an different angle. And so on.

Currently our approach is simple:

  1. AU is always green. If not (not a drama), fix until next day. If there are big refactorings which breaks things for longer, consider feature toggles (anyway a good idea).
  2. ATC = 0/0/*.  Same as above, fix asap.

Everything else (Team velocity, burndown rates etc.pp.) is a different story. Here measuring starts to distort reality even faster. Skip this…

Now for the details. A good ATC variant is key - fine tuning the checks is a special kind of art. That’s a long discussion for itself.

But apart from single findings it’s a good radar, indicating future problem zones. If there is much noise in a certain edge of my system, I know where to look closer at. If I do not, we will see more problems later exactly there.

Number of tests and coverage is - from my experience - not key. They are not an end in itself. If you take them as KPIs, the team will start to write hundreds of meaningless tests 😉 But if you know your system well, e.g. 30% coverage - placed around the critical functionality - is better than 80% missing them. So you have to go thru the AU coverage statistics and judge by yourself. Unfortunately (or luckily?) that cannot be expressed as a KPI.

The lint KPIs etc. may be interesting to see whether tidy up works. Looking at those trends is helpful, do we have the right direction? Also to evaluate quality of a system you are new to.

Famous last words: keep it simple, don’t overengineer measurements, it won’t pay off. Its only one aspect. Trust your senior devs, they know the truth 😎

Happy to hear more opinions,

Holm

entry.title: what does "fra3" in the FQDN stand for?
entry.content:
Dear,
We are troubleshooting an issue and it is unclear what the 'fra3' stands for in the FQDN:
Example:  hecxxvyyyyyy.fra3.sap.<suffix_customer>
We suspect it's not 'Frankfurt' as we see the same 'fra3' at a customer that is residing at Azure Amsterdam... 
And for SAP DC customers (base option), we see 'rot' instead of 'fra3' in the FQDN, most likely standing for datacenter St. Leon Rot in Germany. 
Please explain. 
Thanks, Steven.

entry.title: Re: BTP cl_bali_log_db retention time
entry.content:
Good question, we are also looking into this. 
i guess the jobs are reorganised independently from logs. Log retention time can be set on creation of the log header (like before). Problem will be to have a look at the logs once the job got reorged - and in BTP there is no standalone log app - equivalent to SLG1. That’s why we have created an own one…

entry.title: Re: Decentralized EWM on an SAP S/4HANA stack
entry.content:
Hi,

if I remember correctly (not logged on to an SAP system that late) you use that setting to define that a system is actually used as a decentral EWM.

From your question I am not 100% sure, but I think you are using your system as an ERP system (not a decentralized warehouse management system). If I am right you should not have the setting in a way that defines your system as a decentral EWM - it's either a "central" ERP or it's a decentral EWM, it cannot be both at the same time.

Can you share a screenshot of the setting?

And I agree that your system behaves strangely regarding the posting delays.

Regards Andreas

text="Currency"
press="onLinkPress"
/>

entry.title: Creating a Multi-Item Edit Screen in RAP Similar to SM30
entry.content:
Introduction

In the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP), there's a method to create screens that allow simultaneous editing of multiple rows. Such applications are commonly used to maintain custom master data. This approach is detailed in the following SAP Help documentation:

Developing Transactional Apps with Multi-Inline-Edit Capabilities

How Multiline Editing is Achieved

In a List Report, the table isn't directly editable; instead, you must navigate to the Object Page to edit the table. To enable multiline editing, you need to create a business object with a parent-child relationship, where the child entity is the one being edited. Since only one parent is needed, it's created as a "singleton." Specifically, you retrieve any data source with conditions that always result in a single record and add the most recent update timestamp of the child entity as an ETag field. In the example below, data is fetched from the I_Language view using the login language as the key.

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Carrier Singleton Root View'

define root view entity /DMO/I_CarriersLockSingleton_S
  as select from    I_Language
    left outer join /dmo/carrier as carr on 0 = 0
  composition [0..*] of /DMO/I_Carrier_S as _Airline

{
  key 1                          as CarrierSingletonID,
      max (carr.last_changed_at) as LastChangedAtMax, 

      _Airline
}

where
  I_Language.Language = $session.system_language

 

The most notable difference from a standard RAP BO is that the parent entity is defined as a singleton and set to read-only. Other than that, you add the child entity and define the behavior as usual.

Default Behavior

When following the documentation, the following steps are required to create a new record:

  1. Select a row from the List Report and navigate to the Object Page.
  2. Click the edit button.
  3. Press "Create" to add a row.
  4. Enter the key in the dialog.
  5. Input other fields in the Object Page and press "Apply".

However, this process involves multiple steps, making it quite different from the user experience of SM30.

Objective of This Blog

Building upon the SAP Help example, this blog aims to achieve the following enhancements:

  1. Enable inline creation of rows.
  2. Open the Object Page directly in edit mode, bypassing the List Report.

The first enhancement is achieved through configurations in the Fiori app and the RAP Business Object, while the second is accomplished by setting parameters in SAP Build Work Zone (or target mapping in SAP S/4HANA).

1. Enabling Inline Row Creation

1.1. Set Creation Mode to Inline

To enable inline row creation, select the table from the Page Map and set the Creation Mode to Inline.

However, simply setting it to Inline can result in errors when clicking the "Create" button due to mandatory key fields being empty.

This occurs because the table's key is an input field and is set as "mandatory: create" in the Behavior Definition:

 

field ( mandatory : create, readonly : update ) AirlineID;

 

If marked only as mandatory (not mandatory: create), a draft with a blank key is registered, leading to duplication errors on subsequent entries.

1.2. Use UUID as the Key

To prevent errors, the key should be auto-generated. The following example is not from the SAP Help document, but illustrates a case where the business key (division_code) is user-input, while a technical key using UUID is auto-generated.

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Division Code Master'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zyasu_division {

  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key uuid              : sysuuid_x16 not null;
  division_code         : abap.char(2) not null;
  name                  : text100;
  local_created_by      : abp_creation_user;
  local_created_at      : abp_creation_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_by : abp_locinst_lastchange_user;
  local_last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
  last_changed_at       : abp_lastchange_tstmpl;

}

 

1.3. Auto-Generate UUID and Set Business Key as Mandatory

In the Behavior Definition, set the UUID to numbering: managed and mark the business key as mandatory:

 

field ( numbering: managed, readonly ) ID;
field ( mandatory ) DivisionCode;

 

This ensures that the business key is marked as mandatory, indicated by an asterisk.

1.4. Optional: Validation and Feature Control

Optionally, you can add the following logic:

  • Validation: Trigger an error if mandatory fields are empty upon saving.
  • Feature Control: Make the business key editable only during creation.

2. Opening Object Page Directly in Edit Mode

To open the Object Page directly in without navigating through the List Report, pass the key of the parent entity as a URL parameter when launching the application. Additionally, by specifying preferredMode=edit, the Object Page will open in edit mode.

For example:#?DivisionSingletonID=1&preferredMode=edit

Please refer to the SAPUI5 SDK for details.

The method for achieving this is the same whether you are using SAP Build Work Zone or SAP S/4HANA. However, this section focuses on the procedure for SAP Build Work Zone.

2.1. Configuring Navigation in SAP Build Work Zone

In SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, you can set these parameters in the "Visualization" tab of the application. Since the parent entity always has a single record, you can set a fixed key.

  1. Navigate to the application's "Visualization" tab.
  2. In the parameters section, set the key of the parent entity and preferredMode=edit.

With this configuration, clicking the tile will open the Object Page directly in edit mode.

Conclusion

With this setup, we have successfully created a table update screen similar to SM30.

Additionally, in BTP ABAP Environment and S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there is a similar feature called Business Configuration Maintenance Object, which can also be used for this purpose.

entry.title: Re: Which changes in object(class or function) can case regenerate related code/objects
entry.content: Sorry about my spelling mistakes. my problem is that i release cr to production that for example contain some changes in class A. when the cr uploading it cause to generat exist objects that not exist in the cr( like class and functions) but used class A. so all this objects not available and if i try to run them it will cause dumps. in almost every time i release cr to production all my application not working!, so i think to reduce dependency between objects and i think that if i will know how generating after release cr works i will can isolate some critic feature that still work in the application even in release cr times.

entry.title: Re: Work flow question: How to find who holds a work flow?
entry.content:
Hi sallyxu-cn, you should be able to drill all the way down in the workflow log - whatever starting transaction you used, it doesn't matter - until you see the individual task that is in process.  If you can't, perhaps check with Basis to see if you are missing authorizations?  From the screen shot that you provided, I have annotated it to show you where to drilldown.

Good luck

entry.title: Re: environment variables In SAP Datasphere
entry.content: In what context would you look for such env variables? I am trying to understand the request. Thank you. --Witalij

entry.title: Re: environment variables In SAP Datasphere
entry.content: Hi Witalij, I am creating a data flow from an oData service, but I should not bring 100% of the data from the source system, but only the data for this year, and so on for each new year. I was thinking of using something like ":$CURRENT_YEAR", but I haven't been able to do it. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Reverse Tax Engine - Payroll USA
entry.content:
@CarlosAccorsi 

Hi Carlos -

Could you please check the case 84903/2025 that we have logged?  We have been back and forth on this issue for almost two weeks now and still no resolution.  I have gone over and over this blog post, read all the OSS notes, checked all of our configuration and still nothing works.

entry.title: How add custom field on XML/PDF on a eDocument for Chile?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I need to add a custom field to the XML and PDF of an eDocument for Chile in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. However, I haven't found a specific BAdI for Chile that allows adding custom fields.

I noticed that there is a BAdI called EDOC_MX_FILL_CUSTOM_DATA, which is used for other countries like Mexico to modify the eDocument XML before generation and another BAdI to add this field to PDF also for Mexico.  App Extensibility: eDocument Cockpit 

Would this BAdI work for Chile as well? If not, is there a General BAdI or another recommended approach to add custom fields to the eDocument XML/PDF for Chile?

I appreciate any insights or guidance you can provide.
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Manage KPI Report issue with dynamic passing paramete...
entry.content:
Hi, 

I believe the derivation annotation only works in combination with annotation @consumption.hidden: true.

entry.title: Re: Best practice recommendations on "Save all objects to...
entry.content: Thank you! Good tips. We will try that.

entry.title: Simple Table Maintenance in SAP Fiori Using UI5 Smart Controls
entry.content:
1. What Is a Classic Maintenance View/Table in SAP GUI?

In the SAP GUI world, creating a maintenance view (or table maintenance dialog) is straightforward using transaction-based tools such as SM30. You can generate a maintenance view for a custom table, enabling easy data creation, updates, and deletions. This approach requires minimal coding—SAP generates the necessary screens, logic, and button controls automatically.

Such classic maintenance views are often used by functional teams or administrators to manage configuration tables, text tables, or other small datasets. The big advantage is that it’s a “no-frills” approach — it just works. However, as organizations move to SAP S/4HANA and Fiori, many are looking for a modern UI that is responsive, mobile-friendly, and integrated with Fiori Launchpad.

2. What Are the Current Solutions for It in Fiori with and without RAP?

When transitioning to SAP Fiori, you can no longer rely on SM30-like screens for table maintenance in a straightforward way. Instead you have workarounds like object page editing: Embed an edit-enabled table on an object page to mimic SM30. While this can work, it’s often not the most straightforward approach if your goal is simply to maintain a table of records (see blog post Generating SAP Fiori Table Maintenance Dialogs in SAP S/4HANA 2021.)

3. What Makes My Solution Better?

The solution outlined here is designed with simplicity in mind and takes advantage of the following:

  • OData V2: Avoid mandatory draft handling and simplify immediate data saves.
  • Smart Controls: Declarative UI annotations determine how fields are displayed and edited, minimizing custom coding for the UI layout.
  • Freestyle UI5 App: This approach gives you flexibility to add custom buttons, event handlers, or logic but still using the annotations from the backend

Essentially, you get a classic “table maintenance” style application but with a modern, responsive Fiori interface, plus the flexibility to extend it if your requirements grow.

4. How Does It Work? (With Code Samples)

Below is an overview of the key pieces in this project. The full sample is available on GitHub: https://github.com/marianfoo/fiori-table-maintenance.

4.1 Main View (XML)

The main view is a simple UI5 XML view containing a SmartFilterBar and a SmartTable. With the “editable” property set to true and editTogglable also enabled, the table allows inline editing. We also have buttons for refreshing and saving changes.

 

<mvc:View controllerName="com.starwars.fighter.controller.MainView"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:table="sap.ui.table"
    xmlns:f="sap.f"
    xmlns:smarttable="sap.ui.comp.smarttable"
    xmlns:smartvariants="sap.ui.comp.smartvariants"
    xmlns:smartfilterbar="sap.ui.comp.smartfilterbar"
    xmlns:app="http://schemas.sap.com/sapui5/extension/sap.ui.core.CustomData/1"
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core">
    <Page id="page" title="{i18n>title}">
        <f:DynamicPage id="dynamicPageId" headerExpanded="true" toggleHeaderOnTitleClick="true">
            <f:title>
                <f:DynamicPageTitle>
                    <f:heading>
                        <smartvariants:SmartVariantManagement 
                            id="svm" 
                            persistencyKey="StarWarsFighterVariants"
                            showShare="true"/>
                    </f:heading>
                </f:DynamicPageTitle>
            </f:title>

            <f:header>
                <f:DynamicPageHeader pinnable="true">
                    <smartfilterbar:SmartFilterBar 
                        id="smartFilterBar" 
                        entitySet="StarWarsFighter" 
                        persistencyKey="StarWarsFighterFilter"
                        useToolbar="false">
                    </smartfilterbar:SmartFilterBar>
                </f:DynamicPageHeader>
            </f:header>

            <f:content>
                <smarttable:SmartTable 
                    id="smartTable" 
                    entitySet="StarWarsFighter" 
                    smartFilterId="smartFilterBar"
                    smartVariant="svm"
                    tableType="Table" 
                    enableExport="true"
                    editable="true"
                    editTogglable="true"
                    app:useSmartToggle="true"
                    app:useSmartField="true"
                    beforeExport=".onBeforeExport" 
                    useVariantManagement="true"
                    useTablePersonalisation="true"
                    header="Star Wars Fighters"
                    showRowCount="true"
                    enableAutoColumnWidth="true"
                    enableAutoBinding="true"
                    persistencyKey="StarWarsFighterSmartTable"
                    fieldChange=".onFieldChange"
                    class="sapUiResponsiveMargin">

                    <smarttable:customToolbar>
                        <OverflowToolbar>
                            <ToolbarSpacer />
                            <Button text="Refresh Table" type="Emphasized" press=".onRefreshTable"/>
                            <Button text="Save Changes" type="Emphasized" press=".onSubmitChanges"/>
                        </OverflowToolbar>
                    </smarttable:customToolbar>
                </smarttable:SmartTable>
            </f:content>
        </f:DynamicPage>
    </Page>
</mvc:View>

 

 

4.2 TypeScript Controller

The controller handles basic logic: refreshing the table binding and submitting changes. Notice the use of model.submitChanges() to save modifications to the server.
With the onFieldChange method everytime a user changes a field (and leaves the field or pressing enter) the changes are submitted to the backend.
You can also use the button to only send the changes to the backend when it is pressed.
The UI5 Framework is smart enough to notice only the changes and create batch requests for it.

 

import SmartTable from 'sap/ui/comp/smarttable/SmartTable';
import Controller from 'sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller';
import ODataModel from 'sap/ui/model/odata/v2/ODataModel';
import Table from 'sap/ui/table/Table';

/**
 * @namespace com.starwars.fighter.controller
 */
export default class MainView extends Controller {
  public onInit(): void {
    // Initialization logic (if needed)
  }

  public onRefreshTable(): void {
    const smartTable = this.byId('smartTable') as SmartTable;
    const innerTable = smartTable.getTable() as Table;
    innerTable.getBinding('rows').refresh();
  }

  public onFieldChange(): void {
    const smartTable = this.byId('smartTable') as SmartTable;
    const model = smartTable.getModel() as ODataModel;
    // Immediately submit changes whenever a field is changed
    model.submitChanges();
  }

  public async onSubmitChanges(): Promise {
    const smartTable = this.byId('smartTable') as SmartTable;
    const model = smartTable.getModel() as ODataModel;
    model.submitChanges();
  }
}

 

4.3 Annotations (annotations.cds)

UI Annotations let Smart Controls know which fields to display by default, which fields are editable, and how to label them. I use standard annotations like LineItems and SelectionFields.
In CAP i can use the "Computed" Annotation to make a field in the table not editable.
I made only the status field editable.

 

annotate sw.StarWarsFighter with {
    name @title: 'Fighter Name' .Computed;
    manufacturer @title: 'Manufacturer' .Computed;
);

 

4.4 CAP Schema

The CAP schema (CDS) defines the entities (StarWarsFighter and FighterStatus) and their properties. CAP automatically generates an OData service based on this definition.
With the CAP Plugin @cap-js-community/odata-v2-adapter i can automatically create the V2 Service i need in my app.

 

namespace my.starwars;

entity FighterStatus {
    key code: String;
    name: String;
    description: String;
    isOperational: Boolean;

    fighters: Association to many StarWarsFighter on fighters.status = $self;
}

entity StarWarsFighter {
    key id: String(10);
    name: String;
    manufacturer: String;
    model: String;
    fighterCategory: String;
    builtAt: Timestamp;
    height: Decimal(5,2);
    weight: Decimal(7,2);
    length: Decimal(5,2);
    width: Decimal(5,2);
    crewSize: Integer;
    passengerCapacity: Integer;
    propulsion: String;
    maxAtmosphericSpeed: String;
    maxAcceleration: String;
    hyperdriveClass: String;
    laserCannons: Integer;
    protonTorpedoCapacity: Integer;
    additionalWeapons: String;

    status: Association to FighterStatus;
}

 

 

4.5 Sample Data

Finally, you can load some sample CSV data into the StarWarsFighter entity. Here’s a snippet:

 

id,name,manufacturer,model,fighterCategory,builtAt,height,weight,length,width,crewSize,passengerCapacity,propulsion,maxAtmosphericSpeed,maxAcceleration,hyperdriveClass,laserCannons,protonTorpedoCapacity,additionalWeapons,status_code
SF001,X-wing,Incom Corporation,T-65B,Assault,2020-01-15T10:00:00Z,2.4,10000,12.5,11.76,1,0,Twin ion engines,1050 km/h,3100 G,1.0 HCR,4,6,Deflector shields,AVAILABLE
SF002,TIE Fighter,Sienar Fleet Systems,TIE/ln,Interceptor,2020-02-20T08:30:00Z,7.5,6300,6.3,6.4,1,0,Twin ion engines,1200 km/h,4100 G,0,2,0,None,MISSION
...

 

With all these parts combined, you have a full CAP-based, OData V2 service that your UI5 application (featuring SmartTable) can consume. The end result is a modern UI for table maintenance with immediate saving, filter capabilities, sorting, and variant management—similar to the classic SM30 experience but Fiori-style.

Conclusion

Switching from the “classic” SAP GUI table maintenance to a Fiori-based solution doesn’t have to be complex. By using CAP/RAP Backend, a freestyle UI5 app, and OData V2 (plus UI annotations), you can quickly set up a user-friendly, fully responsive table maintenance application. You get instant saves without the overhead of drafts, plus the flexibility to adapt the UI to more complex scenarios if needed.

Check out the full source and try it out yourself on GitHub: https://github.com/marianfoo/fiori-table-maintenance.

Feel free to leave a comment or ask questions below if you have any feedback or want to share your experiences with table maintenance in Fiori!

entry.title: SAP Global Hierarchy activation impact on existing hierarchies
entry.content:
We are looking to leverage Global Hierarchy Functionality to import GL hierarchy from a different system into SAP. GL Hierarchies maintain in the third-party application is used for cost rollup and business wants us to import those into SAP for cost reports in SAP. 

We are looking to leverage the Global Hierarchy functionality as its easier to maintain and manage. Is there an impact on existing FSV Hierarchies if we activate #GlobalHierarchies . Meaning, can we have both existing FSV (old SAP hierarchy functionality) and new GL Hierarchy via Global Hierarchy concurrently/same time. 

Or do we have to mandatorily convert old FSV hierarchies to new ones? Please advise. 

Appreciate all the help and responses.

entry.title: [ODataMetadata] initial loading of metadata failed - execute locally from VS code
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I developed a Fiori application in Visual Studio code and deployed to SAP system, configured in Fiori launchpad where I am able to run it successfully.

But the same application when executed locally from VS code (http://localhost:8080/index.html?sap-client=400&sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false), I am getting the below error. 

I was not able to figure out what I am missing. Maybe some proxy setup or something? Please let me know. Appreciate your response.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Prevent Occurence of list header and page breaks in spool file of ALV Grid
entry.content: Please see https://me.sap.com/notes/2861244. It is required that you set print parameter 'Spool List with Structure Information' for the job step.

entry.title: Stay certified - Is an SAP Learning Hub subscription required for accessing new learning journeys?
entry.content:
Hi!

I received a notification recently that the C_S4CPB Implementation Consultant—SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition will expire in 8 weeks, and now I am supposed to take the assessment to extend the validity of my certification. However, my SAP Learning Hub subscription has expired, and I only see old learning materials for which I passed the assessment last year (for Cloud version 2402), not the new ones for version 2502. I know that taking the assessment requires an active SAP Learning Hub subscription, but does access to the latest learning journey also require a Learning Hub subscription?

Thanks for any help on this topic.

Mihael

entry.title: Re: how to get spool file from SAP (not using SM37)
entry.content: SP01 is the transaction you can use to view all spool requests. With report BTCAUX05, you can check by which jobs spool requests have been created.

entry.title: Re: Which interpretation algorithm is used to extract the...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

yes so what you are looking for are the interpretation algorithms. You find the overview here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/3cb1182b4a184bdd93f8d62e3f1f0741/4dc5efb40c381c36e10000000a42189b.html

 

In the old world you defined a posting rule for a specific external transaction, which would apply the algorithm which you have maintained in the rule. 

In the new world, you create a processing rule, which has action Type "Clear in Sequence" which applies a processing instruction (which does the same in the end, depending on the algorithm behind it)

 

More information here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/3cb1182b4a184bdd93f8d62e3f1f0741/ed31be4969ce485085da152c3a3e8062.html

 

Hope this explains everything. 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: SAP Desktop Agent is not running in unattended mode
entry.content:
What is the trigger configuration that you have done for the automation.

Please share a screenshot for the same.

Right now, based on the details shared by you, doesn't look like a scheduled/api based trigger has been created for the automation.

entry.title: Re: How can I extract the reference number from the <Ustr...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

what you try to do is currently not possible. But we work on something which might help in the future. So stay tuned and read the What's News.

Now for your case, usually you would simply map the data which the bank sends to these fields 1:1. Then you execute some of the interpretation algorithms, which would extract the reference number from the Note to Payee field. 

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: sap gui gesperrt nach mehreren Fehlversuchen sich anzumelden
entry.content:
Hallo,

ich habe es so versucht wie Sie beschrieben haben, aber bei mir kommt noch immer der Fehlermeldung

"Kennwortanmeldung nicht mehr möglich- zu viele Fehlversuche", Ich bitte um Hilfe wie ich mich hier anmelden könnte.

Vielen Dank!

entry.title: Re: Unable to find SubTotal function in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: I could not find the subtotal function either, only the grandtotal function is available.

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi, did you create the event handler class under a Standard ABAP package or ABAP Cloud Package? Is it still considered as clean core even though we created the class under the Standard ABAP package? 

@Andre_Fischer , any thoughts?

entry.title: Re: SAC Live Connection Schedule Publication not working
entry.content: As schedule publication on live connection requires 'Advance feature' setting in connection or tunnel connection, probably it worth to check the connection setting as well as the cloud connector.

entry.title: Re: Effective date when status is "Inactive" for a Depart...
entry.content:
Hi R,

Could you please try to add a custom field (end date) on object Department? And configure a rule like: if jobInfo.department.value.enddate is equal to date plus () (today plus certain days) , then raise message.

Regards,

Kayla

entry.title: Activity Rate planning
entry.content:
Dear PERSON_NAME,
Could you please explain how to manage activity rates in planning concerning cost centers in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Fiori app? We have checked the Fiori Reference Library but could not find any relevant app.
In other private cloud or ECC systems, we typically use transaction codes KP26 and KBK6 to perform this task.
Looking forward to your guidance.
 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Activity Rate planning
entry.content:
Kindly use below apps for cost rate planning, see

Manage Cost Rates - Plan

Manage Cost Rates - Services

Hope above information helps.

entry.title: Re: Difference between job type "Synch HRIS data" and "HR...
entry.content:
Hi,

There is not much difference between the two job types. Sync HRIS Data job is scheduled in Scheduled Job Manager in the tenant while HRIS Sync job is scheduled in provisioning. And there's more Job Parameters can be configured in HRIS Sync job in the provisioning. For example, Compensation specific options for HRIS sync job, Sync job runtime options. Hope this clarifies. 

Regards,

Kayla

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors EC. Position Management change manager...
entry.content: Hi, you can update position->department via job info to position sync.

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors EC. Position Management change manager...
entry.content: Hi, you can update position->department via job info to position sync but manager change in job info cannot be updated to position by changing the parent position. This can only be done in the opposition direction. Namely, update manager based on parent position change via position to job info sync.

entry.title: Re: Access Denied on project in SAP Build Apps
entry.content: Hi, I have similar issues for past 2 days. could you please check on this?

entry.title: Re: User Info portlet in the Latest People Profile
entry.content:
Hi,

Unfortunately, USERINFO_BLOCK is not migrated to Latest People Profile. Please refer to the below content:

 https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3529276#:~:text=Attempt%20to%20add%20this%20standard%20block%20in%20the,The%20USERINFO_BLOCK%20is%20mainly%20for%20non-Employee%20Central%20customers.

Best regards,

Elsa

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @RavdeepSingh ,

Whether this event will trigger via GUI t-code(va01) or it is not possible for GUI t-code.

Whether this event will trigger only when we use the respective RAP based fiori app where the Event is declared 

Please suggest.

Thanks

entry.title: Facing below error Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Generic_Object_Query
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

I am facing below error in Standard Replication iFlow in SAP CPI.

Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Generic_Object_Query failed with message "NullPointerException:while trying to invoke the method org.apache.catalina.TomcatPrincipal.getUserPrincipal() of a null object loaded from field org.apache.catalina.connector.Request.userPrincipal of an object loaded from local variable 'this'"

Please advice on the root cause and any steps need to be taken?

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors EC. Position Management change manager...
entry.content:
I did have the same question as you. I get the answer after reading
1. 2636185 - Move Position to New Supervisor on Job Info Change - EC
2. 2598479 - Job to Position Sync Doesn't Synchronize Manager and Field Values in Same Transaction

However, it is annoying that either a manager change or another field data change is only effective if both happen in the same transaction.

So it can't fully meet your requirements. Finally, I use the integration center to enhance job info and position sync.

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content: I think it will trigger from both. You can do a where-used of the event in the RAP BO. Another way to check is to implement the event handler class and maybe have a breakpoint.

entry.title: Re: Consuming a released event of an unreleased RAP BO (h...
entry.content: Hi, there is an event for our requirement. Our question is whether consuming these events locally is classified as Tier 1. Currently, to consume the events, we need to create the class under the Standard ABAP package in S4H 2023 PCE.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Return Not inspected
entry.content: Hi, have you solved this problem? I've meet the same problem.

entry.title: Re: Mass Data Updates from Excel in UI5 and Fiori elements with this Open Source Project
entry.content:
Hi Marian,

This is great and works like a charm. RAP excel upload for data maintenance had been missing something like this. I do have a question around the component usage though, do you think we will have to specify a version every time as I see on github now there is a V1_7_3 already.

v1_7_2

Thanks,

Rahul

entry.title: Re: EWM Layout-Oriented Storage Control (LOSC) – A simple guide
entry.content: Excellent content @Yuri_Nogueira , thanks for sharing!

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @Ramnnk ,

I have tried it. it didn't trigger via GUI.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to create a custom field with Custom Field and Logic App and transporting field in SAP S/4H
entry.content: Is there any way we can group the custom fields and sectionor description  to it ?
